data.narrative:
Newspace Research is a Series B-stage defense and security company that has raised $73.2M to date. Valuation analysis relies on a universe of 10 comparables—2 public firms (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers—all operating within frontier defense technology. The private peer set includes Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns, all at the same stage.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the company's $73M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implying a value of $139M. Peer-median analysis examined six same-sector, same-stage comparables with valuations ranging from $1.0B to $1.5B, yielding a median of $1.2B. This wide range reflects heterogeneity in the private defense-tech landscape at Series B maturity.

The triangulated analysis produces a low bound of $118M, a median of $670M, and a high bound of $1.4B. The spread between methods reflects material uncertainty about whether Newspace Research should be valued closer to its capital deployed or to the median of better-capitalized peers. Key uncertainties include revenue traction relative to peer cohort, technology differentiation within the defense sector, and customer concentration risk. Additional clarity on revenue run-rate and contract pipeline would narrow this range materially.
